title: My Neighbourï؟½s Garden
content:
آ آ آ آ  Why in my neighbourï؟½s garden
آ آ آ آ  Are the flowers more sweet than mine?
آ آ آ آ  I had never such bloom of roses,
آ آ آ آ  Such yellow and pink woodbine.

آ آ آ آ  Why in my neighbourï؟½s garden
آ آ آ آ  Are the fruits all red and gold,
آ آ آ آ  While here the grapes are bitter
آ آ آ آ  That hang for my fingersï؟½ hold?

آ آ آ آ  Why in my neighbourï؟½s garden
آ آ آ آ  Do the birds all fly to sing?
آ آ آ آ  Over the fence between us
آ آ آ آ  One would think ï؟½twas always spring.

آ آ آ آ  I thought my own wide garden
آ آ آ آ  Once more sweet and fair than all,
آ آ آ آ  Till I saw the gold and crimson
آ آ آ آ  Just over my neighbourï؟½s wall.

آ آ آ آ  But now I want his thrushes,
آ آ آ آ  And now I want his vine,
آ آ آ آ  If I cannot have his cherries
آ آ آ آ  That grow more red than mine.

آ آ آ آ  The serpent ï؟½neath his apples
آ آ آ آ  Will tempt me to my fall,
آ آ آ آ  And then-Iï؟½ll steal my neighbourï؟½s fruit
آ آ آ آ  Across the garden wall.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Dora Sigerson Shorter, titled "My Neighbourï؟½s Garden", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
